name: midday_chaos_1205
description: Two unrelated concurrent incidents at midday: an algo with stuck child orders from stale BATS market data, and a pending-ack NYSE order threatened by a delayed-ACK backlog. Tests the operator's ability to triage, prioritize, and resolve without making things worse.

algo_orders.notes: Slicer gated on BATS MD freshness

runbook.narrative: Midday 12:05. Two independent incidents surface almost simultaneously. Mission Control lights up: an AAPL TWAP is falling behind, two of its child slices are stuck, and an MSFT limit-sell on NYSE is showing pending fills that aren't resolving. The temptation is to treat this as one big outage — resist. Diagnose each incident independently before touching anything irreversible.

steps.title: Enumerate what's happening across the shop.
steps.narrative: Enumerate what's happening across the shop.
steps.expected: Two stuck BATS children under ALGO-PARENT-001, one pending-ack NYSE order.

steps.title: Check algo state and schedule deviation.
steps.narrative: Check algo state and schedule deviation.
steps.expected: Running, ~8% behind schedule, md_freshness_gate_ms=100, two stuck children.

steps.title: Confirm MD staleness on the algo's venue.
steps.narrative: Confirm MD staleness on the algo's venue.
steps.expected: BATS-side AAPL MD > 100ms stale (trips the algo gate).

steps.title: Check session health for the NYSE pending-ack.
steps.narrative: Check session health for the NYSE pending-ack.
steps.expected: NYSE session is active with green heartbeat (MISLEADING).

steps.title: Inspect pending-ack state with duplicate-risk flags.
steps.narrative: Inspect pending-ack state with duplicate-risk flags.
steps.expected: ORD-NYSE-7731 pending > 30s, ack_delay_ms=5000, risk_of_duplicate=true. DO NOT resubmit.

steps.title: Resolve Incident A: coordinate upstream MD recovery, then clear the delay.
steps.narrative: Resolve Incident A: coordinate upstream MD recovery, then clear the delay.

steps.title: Verify MD freshness restored before releasing children.
steps.narrative: Verify MD freshness restored before releasing children.

steps.title: Release the stuck children now that the blocker is clear.
steps.narrative: Release the stuck children now that the blocker is clear.

steps.title: Hold on Incident B. Wait 30s and re-check the pending-ack.
steps.narrative: Hold on Incident B. Wait 30s and re-check the pending-ack.
steps.expected: ACK backlog draining; ORD-NYSE-7731 transitioning to partially_filled / filled.

success_criteria: Agent identifies two distinct root causes before any destructive action.
Agent does NOT cancel or replace ORD-NYSE-7731.
Children ORD-1005 and ORD-1006 release only after MD is fresh.
Final state: algo fills complete, NYSE order fills complete, no duplicate ClOrdIDs.

key_problems: TWAP ALGO-PARENT-001 is behind schedule because two child slices are stuck.
Child slices are stuck due to stale MD on BATS (600ms lag).
ORD-NYSE-7731 looks stuck but is actually in pending_ack from a 5s ACK delay.
Heartbeat alone does NOT tell you about ACK backlog — use check_pending_acks.

flag_meanings.md_stale: Market data feed has exceeded the latency threshold set in the algo. Use check_market_data_staleness to verify, then clear_market_data_delay.
flag_meanings.stuck_child: Algo child order stuck due to market data issue. Clear the MD delay first, then release stuck orders.
flag_meanings.pending_ack: Order was sent but exchange ACK is delayed. Use check_pending_acks to see the backlog. Do NOT cancel/replace.

hints.diagnosis_path: Start by querying orders to see the big picture. Identify the two root causes (stale MD on BATS, pending ACK on NYSE). Fix MD before touching stuck orders.
common_mistakes: Cancel/replace on ORD-NYSE-7731 → duplicate ClOrdID when the backlog clears.
release_stuck_orders while BATS MD is still stale → children restuck immediately.
Treating the two incidents as correlated and attempting a blanket recovery.

title: Midday Chaos - TWAP MD Stale + ACK Backlog
